questions.question: What is the default value of the CSS overflow property?
questions.explanation: The default value of overflow is visible, meaning content that overflows the box will be rendered outside the element's boundaries.

questions.question: Which overflow value always shows scrollbars regardless of content size?
questions.explanation: overflow: scroll always displays scrollbars, even if the content fits within the container.

questions.question: What does overflow: hidden do?
options: Hides scrollbars but keeps scrolling functional
questions.explanation: overflow: hidden clips any content that overflows the box and does not provide scrollbars to access the clipped content.

questions.question: Which overflow value only shows scrollbars when content overflows?
questions.explanation: overflow: auto shows scrollbars only when the content exceeds the container dimensions, keeping the interface clean when content fits.

questions.question: What three CSS properties are needed to create a text ellipsis?
questions.explanation: Creating a text ellipsis requires: white-space: nowrap (prevents wrapping), overflow: hidden (clips excess), and text-overflow: ellipsis (adds ...).

questions.question: What is the difference between overflow: hidden and overflow: clip?
options: Clip prevents programmatic scrolling, hidden doesn't
Hidden prevents programmatic scrolling, clip doesn't
Clip only works on block elements
questions.explanation: Both clip content visually, but clip also prevents programmatic scrolling (e.g., via JavaScript scrollTop).

questions.question: Which property can be used to prevent scroll chaining in nested scrollable areas?
questions.explanation: overscroll-behavior: contain prevents scroll chaining, where scrolling past the boundary of one element scrolls the parent element.

questions.question: What happens when overflow-x is set to scroll and overflow-y is set to visible?
options: overflow-y is forced to scroll or auto
questions.explanation: If one axis is set to visible and the other to a non-visible value, the visible value is forced to auto/scroll to avoid negative margin collapsing issues.

questions.question: Which property helps with breaking long words that cause overflow?
questions.explanation: overflow-wrap: break-word (or anywhere) allows long words to break and wrap to the next line, preventing horizontal overflow.

questions.question: How can you make an image responsive and prevent overflow?
questions.explanation: max-width: 100% ensures the image never exceeds its container width while maintaining aspect ratio when combined with height: auto.

questions.question: What does scrollbar-gutter property do?
options: Reserves space for scrollbar to prevent layout shift
questions.explanation: scrollbar-gutter reserves space for the scrollbar even when it's not visible, preventing content from shifting when scrollbars appear/disappear.

questions.question: Why might position: sticky not work inside an overflow: hidden container?
options: Sticky doesn't work with hidden overflow
The container clips the sticky behavior
questions.explanation: Sticky positioning doesn't work inside containers with overflow: hidden, auto, or scroll because the overflow creates a new scroll container.

questions.question: Which CSS property controls whether scroll behavior is smooth or instant?
questions.explanation: scroll-behavior: smooth enables smooth scrolling for anchor links and scroll operations, while auto provides instant jumping.

questions.question: What is a common cause of unexpected horizontal scrollbars on web pages?
options: An element with fixed width exceeding viewport
questions.explanation: Fixed-width elements that are wider than the viewport cause horizontal scrollbars. Using max-width instead of width prevents this.

questions.question: How do you create a scrollable container that is keyboard accessible?
questions.explanation: Adding tabindex="0" makes a scrollable div focusable via keyboard, allowing users to use arrow keys to scroll within it.

questions.question: What does the contain property with value paint do for overflow?
options: Improves paint performance by clipping overflow
Creates a paint effect on overflow
questions.explanation: contain: paint acts similarly to overflow: hidden but is a performance optimization that tells the browser the element's children won't paint outside its bounds.

questions.question: Which value of overflow-wrap allows breaking in the middle of a word?
questions.explanation: overflow-wrap: anywhere allows the browser to break a word at any point if needed. break-word only breaks if the entire word doesn't fit.

questions.question: What happens to margin collapse with overflow: auto or hidden?
questions.explanation: Setting overflow to anything other than visible (e.g., auto or hidden) on an element prevents its margins from collapsing with its children.

questions.question: Which is the most performant overflow value for paint?
questions.explanation: overflow: hidden can improve performance by reducing the area the browser needs to paint, as it knows content won't paint outside the box.

questions.question: What is the recommended ARIA role for a scrollable container?
questions.explanation: role="region" with an aria-label helps screen reader users identify and navigate to scrollable areas on the page.
